touches.ai_summary: Cold email sent to Aisha Agarwal introducing AskElephant's revenue intelligence platform and highlighting forecasting accuracy improvements for mid-market SaaS teams. No response received.
Follow-up email sent to Aisha with case study from similar vertical SaaS company showing 34% improvement in forecast accuracy. No response received.

Brief cold call with Aisha. She mentioned recently starting as Head of Sales at OakLogic and expressed interest in tools to improve her team's forecasting. Agreed to a longer qualification call next week.

questions_asked.question: What are your current pain points with revenue forecasting?
questions_asked.answer: We're missing our forecasts by 15-20% most quarters. I just started here and need to get this under control.

touches.ai_summary: Qualification call with Aisha. Discussed OakLogic's current forecasting process, team size, and evaluation timeline. Aisha mentioned they're also looking at Chorus but is concerned about implementation complexity. She confirmed budget authority and Q2 evaluation timeline.

questions_asked.question: What tools are you currently using for call analysis and forecasting?
questions_asked.answer: We're mostly manual right now - reps update Salesforce, but there's no automated call intelligence. We've seen demos of Chorus.

questions_asked.question: How does your implementation compare to Chorus in terms of time and complexity?
questions_asked.answer: We typically deploy in 2-3 weeks vs 6-8 weeks. Our unlimited automation tier doesn't require professional services.

next_steps: Intro to RevOps team for technical fit

touches.ai_summary: Email sent with pricing overview for unlimited automation tier at 29 seats. Included ROI calculator and implementation timeline. Aisha responded asking to include Fatima Thomas from RevOps in next conversation.

questions_asked.question: Can you walk us through the data integration requirements with Salesforce?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, I'll have our solutions engineer join the discovery call to cover the technical architecture.

touches.ai_summary: Sent calendar invite for discovery call including Aisha and Fatima. Attached technical overview deck covering Salesforce integration, data security, and automation capabilities. Aisha confirmed attendance.

Brief check-in call with Aisha before discovery meeting. She mentioned getting pressure from leadership to improve forecast accuracy after a rough Q1. Expressed urgency but noted she needs to bring procurement and technical evaluators into the process.

questions_asked.question: Who else needs to be involved in the evaluation decision?
questions_asked.answer: Ravi Patel will do technical eval, and Scarlett White handles procurement. I can champion this but don't have final sign-off on new tools yet.

objections: Need to involve procurement and get multi-stakeholder buy-in
next_steps: Plan for technical eval with Ravi

touches.ai_summary: Discovery call with Aisha and Fatima. Covered current forecasting workflow, pain points with manual Salesforce updates, and requirements for automated call scoring. Fatima focused on data pipeline and reporting needs. Both expressed interest but Fatima raised questions about Chorus comparison.

questions_asked.question: What's your differentiation from Chorus on the forecasting accuracy side?
questions_asked.answer: We use deal-level risk calibration with historical outcomes, not just call sentiment. Our customers see 25-30% better accuracy.

questions_asked.question: What's your current forecast miss rate and what would success look like?
questions_asked.answer: We're at 15-20% miss rate. Getting under 10% would be a huge win for leadership confidence.

touches.ai_summary: Technical evaluation call with Ravi Patel (rescheduled once due to conflict). Deep dive on API architecture, data models, HNSW vector retrieval for similar deal matching, and XGBoost scoring methodology. Ravi asked detailed questions about model performance and expressed cautious interest but noted Chorus has stronger brand recognition in their network.

questions_asked.question: What's your model's precision-recall on win/loss prediction?
questions_asked.answer: We're at 0.76 F1 score on validation set with 30-day lookahead. Happy to share the methodology deck.

questions_asked.question: How do you handle data drift as our business changes?
questions_asked.answer: Models retrain monthly on your closed deals. We also surface when prediction confidence drops for manual review.

questions_asked.question: What's your current tech stack for data integration?
questions_asked.answer: Salesforce, Outreach, some Gong recordings. We'd need to migrate call data over.

objections: Chorus is more established and has better brand recognition in the market

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email to Aisha with technical docs Ravi requested, plus Chorus head-to-head comparison on implementation speed and unlimited automation tier. Aisha responded that Ravi's feedback was positive on technical fit but they need to see a full demo before moving forward.

questions_asked.question: Can we see the forecasting dashboard in action with real deal data?
questions_asked.answer: Absolutely - I'll set up a custom demo environment with anonymized data from a similar vertical SaaS company.

next_steps: Schedule demo with Aisha and team

touches.ai_summary: Full product demo with Aisha, Fatima, and two other RevOps analysts. Showed live forecasting dashboard, deal risk scoring, and automated call summarization. Team engaged with questions about rollout plan and user adoption. Aisha mentioned they're still evaluating Chorus in parallel but AskElephant's automation tier is more appealing for their team size.

questions_asked.question: What's the typical user adoption curve for a 29-seat deployment?
questions_asked.answer: Most teams hit 80% daily active usage within 4 weeks with our onboarding playbook.

questions_asked.question: How customizable are the risk scoring weights for our specific business?
questions_asked.answer: Fully customizable - you can adjust feature importance and add custom signals during onboarding.

touches.ai_summary: Brief call with Aisha to confirm proposal parameters before sending. She confirmed 29 seats, unlimited automation tier, and Q2 start date. Mentioned she needs to get Scarlett from procurement involved for contract review.

questions_asked.question: What's your approval process for a $24K annual contract?
questions_asked.answer: I can champion it but Scarlett needs to review terms and we'll likely need a final exec sign-off. I don't have full authority yet.

touches.ai_summary: Proposal review call with Aisha and Scarlett. Walked through pricing, terms, implementation timeline, and support SLAs. Scarlett focused on contract length, cancellation terms, and data ownership. Aisha expressed strong interest but noted she needs exec-level buy-in before proceeding. No exec sponsor identified yet.

questions_asked.question: What are your contract length options and early termination terms?
questions_asked.answer: Standard is 12-month with 60-day termination notice. We can discuss quarterly billing if that helps.

questions_asked.question: Who from the leadership team should we loop in for final approval?
questions_asked.answer: Probably our CRO, but I need to coordinate timing. Let me check on their availability.

next_steps: Revise proposal with quarterly billing option

touches.ai_summary: Executive alignment meeting with Aisha and David Park (RevOps Analyst attending for exec). CRO was not available. David asked questions about ROI timeline and competitive landscape. Expressed concern about overlap with Chorus evaluation. Meeting felt more like an internal check than exec sponsorship.

questions_asked.question: What's the typical ROI timeline for forecast accuracy improvements?
questions_asked.answer: Most customers see measurable improvement in 60-90 days once the model has 2 months of data.

questions_asked.question: How does your pricing compare to Chorus for our team size?
questions_asked.answer: Chorus would be about 35% higher for equivalent features, and their automation tier is capped at 20 workflows.

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email to Aisha requesting confirmed time with CRO for exec sponsorship. Also sent updated proposal with quarterly billing option and extended Chorus comparison. Aisha responded that she's working on CRO calendar but they're evaluating both vendors in parallel.

Email from Aisha indicating they've received final pricing from Chorus and are now in active comparison mode. She asked for our best and final pricing and whether we can offer any additional discount for Q2 close. Tone suggests budget pressure and competitive dynamics in play.

questions_asked.question: Is there flexibility on the $24K price if we commit to annual prepay?
questions_asked.answer: We can offer 10% discount for annual prepay, bringing it to $21,600.

touches.ai_summary: Negotiation call with Aisha, Scarlett, and finally the CRO joining for 15 minutes. CRO asked high-level questions about platform vision and forecast methodology but did not express strong conviction. Aisha pushed for further discount to match Chorus. Discussed implementation timeline and success metrics. CRO delegated final decision back to Aisha and Scarlett.

questions_asked.question: What's your product roadmap for the next 12 months?
questions_asked.answer: We're adding multi-modal signal processing and expanding integrations to Slack and Teams for conversation intelligence.

questions_asked.question: Can you match Chorus pricing if we move forward this week?
questions_asked.answer: We've already offered 10% discount. I can check with leadership on additional flexibility but can't commit here.

questions_asked.question: If we finalize terms, what's your ideal kickoff date?
questions_asked.answer: Early May would be ideal to get data flowing before Q3 planning.

objections: Pricing needs to be more competitive with Chorus

touches.ai_summary: Pricing negotiation call with Aisha and Scarlett. Offered additional 5% discount (15% total) to bring ACV to $20,400 for annual prepay and Q2 close. Aisha appreciated the movement but said they need a few days to finalize decision between AskElephant and Chorus. Scarlett confirmed terms are acceptable pending final vendor selection.

questions_asked.question: What's your decision timeline and can we help with anything else to support the evaluation?
questions_asked.answer: We'll make a call by end of week. The pricing is competitive now - it's really about implementation confidence and long-term roadmap fit.
